summary: Executed backend+frontend validation for Sprint 2 Phase 2 (Campaign Builder AI optimization) against configured public URL. Runtime is blocked by deployment mismatch: requested /api/campaigns and AI routes are not exposed, and frontend URL serves Emergent placeholder instead of WhatCEM.

critical.issue: Returns 404 on configured public URL; alias endpoint not routed in runtime.
Returns 404 on configured public URL; cannot validate optimizedContent/score/variations response.
Returns 404 on configured public URL; cannot validate variation list payload.
Returns 404 on configured public URL; cannot validate recommendedDatetimeIso/rationale payload.
Returns 404 on configured public URL; regression flow for save draft blocked.
Returns 404 on configured public URL; regression flow for launch campaign blocked.

ui_bugs.issue: https://dragon-core.preview.emergentagent.com renders generic 'Emergent | Fullstack App' placeholder, not WhatCEM Campaign Builder UI.

integration_issues.flow: Campaign Builder IA controls (optimizar/generar variaciones/recomendar horario) + draft/launch regression
integration_issues.issue: Could not execute UI integration flows because target runtime does not expose WhatCEM frontend or campaigns backend routes.

action_items: Deploy/runtime-sync WhatCEM build that includes /api/campaigns AI routes and Campaign Builder UI to configured public URL.
Update /app/frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to the actual WhatCEM public environment for this repo iteration, then rerun backend+UI tests.
After runtime alignment, retest full requested scope including response shapes (optimizedContent/score/variations, recommendedDatetimeIso/rationale) and end-to-end draft/launch flows.
critical_code_review_comments: Source/runtime inconsistency: server/routes/campaigns.ts clearly defines the requested endpoints, but deployed runtime returns 404 for all of them.
CampaignBuilder.tsx is very large (>700 lines) and should be split into focused modules to reduce regression risk and improve maintainability.
In recommendScheduleWithAI, converting recommended datetime via new Date(recommendedIso).toISOString().slice(0,16) can shift timezone unexpectedly for datetime-local inputs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Read /app/memory/test_credentials.md first (contains app.whatcem.com admin credentials). Testing on configured URL (/app/frontend/.env -> dragon-core.preview.emergentagent.com) currently hits non-WhatCEM placeholder and all /api/campaigns* endpoints return 404. Start next run by validating deployment URL alignment before attempting auth/UI deep tests.
rca of the issue: Root cause is deployment/environment mismatch, not endpoint implementation in source. Reproduction: run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_campaign_ai_public_endpoints.py against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env -> all requested /api/campaigns* routes return 404; open same URL in browser -> placeholder app ('Emergent | Fullstack App'). Mitigation: point configured public URL to deployed WhatCEM build containing this repo changes, then rerun full backend and frontend integration coverage.
test_credentials: Read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md: admin@whatcem.com / Sophia21# (not used because runtime mismatch blocked auth flow execution).
